# Log-Mean Temperature Difference — Interactive Demo {.unnumbered}

When a fluid flows through a heat exchanger, the temperature difference between the hot and cold streams changes along the length. The local driving force for heat transfer is the local $\Delta T$, so we can't just use the inlet or outlet difference as the "effective" average — we need the **log-mean temperature difference**:

$$\Delta T_{lm} = \frac{\Delta T_1 - \Delta T_2}{\ln(\Delta T_1/\Delta T_2)}$$

where $\Delta T_1$ and $\Delta T_2$ are the temperature differences at the two ends of the exchanger (hot-inlet end and hot-outlet end, respectively).

The temperature profiles along the exchanger are exponential (not linear) determined by the heat capacity rates $C = \dot{m}c_p$ of each stream. Given the four terminal temperatures, those profiles are fully determined -- that's what the demo plots.


::: {.callout-note}
Where this fits right now: In the bank of tubes section of Chapter 4, the hot side is a surface held at constant temperature $T_s$. This is the default setting here, but the demo also includes counter- and parallel-flow arrangements, which are heat exchanger concepts coming in a few weeks. For now, just use "Constant $T_s$ (tube bank)".
:::

## Interactive demo {#sec-demo}

```{ojs}
//| echo: false

{
  // ── Profile helper ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  // f(ξ, R): exponential interpolation from 0 to 1 over ξ ∈ [0,1].
  // R = ln(ΔT₁/ΔT₂). R=0 → linear (constant ΔT, balanced capacity rates).
  function fpro(xi, R) {
    if (Math.abs(R) < 1e-8) return xi;
    return (1 - Math.exp(-R * xi)) / (1 - Math.exp(-R));
  }

  // ── Inputs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const flow_in  = Inputs.select(["Constant Tₛ (tube bank)", "Counter flow", "Parallel flow"],
                                  { label: "Flow arrangement", value: "Constant Tₛ (tube bank)" });

  const Thin_in  = Inputs.range([20, 200], { value: 90, step: 1,
                                             label: html`T<sub>h,in</sub> (°C)` });
  const Thout_in = Inputs.range([10, 195], { value: 60, step: 1,
                                             label: html`T<sub>h,out</sub> (°C)` });
  const Tcin_in  = Inputs.range([5,  150], { value: 20, step: 1,
                                             label: html`T<sub>c,in</sub> (°C)` });
  const Tcout_in = Inputs.range([5,  155], { value: 55, step: 1,
                                             label: html`T<sub>c,out</sub> (°C)` });

  // ── Isothermal checkboxes ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  function makeCheck(lbl) {
    const wrap = document.createElement("label");
    wrap.style.cssText = "display:block;font-family:Georgia,serif;font-size:11px;" +
                         "color:#666;margin-top:6px;cursor:pointer;user-select:none";
    const cb = document.createElement("input");
    cb.type = "checkbox";
    cb.style.cssText = "margin-right:5px;cursor:pointer";
    wrap.append(cb, lbl);
    return wrap;
  }
  const hIsoCb = makeCheck("Isothermal  (T₀ᵤₜ = Tᵢⁿ)");
  const cIsoCb = makeCheck("Isothermal  (T₀ᵤₜ = Tᵢⁿ)");

  // ── Plot placeholder ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const pPlot = document.createElement("div");
  const pInfo = document.createElement("div");
  const hotPanelLabel = document.createElement("div");
  hotPanelLabel.style.cssText = "font-size:0.72em;font-weight:bold;color:#c0392b;" +
                                 "text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;margin-bottom:7px";

  // ── Render ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  function render() {
    const isConstantTs = flow_in.value === "Constant Tₛ (tube bank)";
    const isCounter    = flow_in.value === "Counter flow";

    hotPanelLabel.textContent = isConstantTs ? "Tube surface (Tₛ = const.)" : "Hot stream";

    const Thin  = Thin_in.value;
    const hIsoChecked = isConstantTs || hIsoCb.querySelector("input").checked;
    const Thout = hIsoChecked ? Thin : Thout_in.value;
    const Tcin  = Tcin_in.value;
    const Tcout = cIsoCb.querySelector("input").checked ? Tcin  : Tcout_in.value;

    // Dim/lock disabled sliders
    for (const [el, iso] of [[Thout_in, hIsoCb], [Tcout_in, cIsoCb]]) {
      const on = iso.querySelector("input").checked;
      el.style.opacity       = on ? "0.35" : "1";
      el.style.pointerEvents = on ? "none"  : "";
    }
    if (isConstantTs) {
      Thout_in.style.opacity                    = "0.35";
      Thout_in.style.pointerEvents              = "none";
      hIsoCb.querySelector("input").checked     = true;
      hIsoCb.style.opacity                      = "0.4";
      hIsoCb.style.pointerEvents                = "none";
    } else {
      hIsoCb.style.opacity       = "1";
      hIsoCb.style.pointerEvents = "";
    }

    // Cold temperature at ξ=0 (hot-inlet end) and ξ=1 (hot-outlet end)
    // Counter flow: cold exits at ξ=0, enters at ξ=1
    // Parallel flow: cold enters at ξ=0, exits at ξ=1
    const [Tc0, Tc1] = isCounter ? [Tcout, Tcin] : [Tcin, Tcout];

    const DT1 = Thin  - Tc0;   // ΔT at hot-inlet end
    const DT2 = Thout - Tc1;   // ΔT at hot-outlet end
    const valid = DT1 > 0.1 && DT2 > 0.1;

    const q_h = Thin  - Thout;   // hot-side temperature drop
    const q_c = Tc0   - Tc1;     // cold-side temperature change at ξ (+ means drops)
    const R   = (valid && Math.abs(DT1 - DT2) > 0.05) ? Math.log(DT1 / DT2) : 0;

    // LMTD (L'Hôpital when R→0)
    const DT_lm    = Math.abs(R) < 1e-8 ? DT1 : (DT1 - DT2) / Math.log(DT1 / DT2);
    const DT_arith = (DT1 + DT2) / 2;

    // Profile rows
    const N = 300;
    const xs = Array.from({length: N}, (_, i) => i / (N - 1));
    const rows = xs.map(xi => ({
      xi,
      Th: Thin - q_h * fpro(xi, R),
      Tc: Tc0  - q_c * fpro(xi, R),
    }));

    const allT = rows.flatMap(r => [r.Th, r.Tc]);
    const Tlo  = Math.min(...allT) - 8;
    const Thi  = Math.max(...allT) + 8;
    const arrowOff = (Thi - Tlo) * 0.05;   // float arrows above/below the profile lines

    // Arrow mid-points
    const Th_mid = Thin - q_h * fpro(0.5, R);
    const Tc_mid = Tc0  - q_c * fpro(0.5, R);

    const plot = Plot.plot({
      width: 680, height: 320,
      marginLeft: 52, marginBottom: 42, marginTop: 24, marginRight: 24,
      style: { fontFamily: "Georgia, serif", fontSize: "11px" },
      x: { label: "← hot inlet · · · · · hot outlet →", domain: [0, 1],
           tickFormat: d => ["0","¼","½","¾","1"][Math.round(d * 4)] },
      y: { label: "↑ T (°C)", domain: [Tlo, Thi] },
      marks: valid ? [
        Plot.gridX({ stroke: "#e0e0e0" }),
        Plot.gridY({ stroke: "#e0e0e0" }),

        // Temperature profiles
        Plot.line(rows, { x: "xi", y: "Th", stroke: "#c0392b", strokeWidth: 2.5 }),
        Plot.line(rows, { x: "xi", y: "Tc", stroke: "#2471a3", strokeWidth: 2.5 }),

        // Flow-direction arrows (floated above/below the profile lines)
        Plot.arrow([{ x1: 0.38, y1: Th_mid + arrowOff, x2: 0.52, y2: Th_mid + arrowOff }],
          { x1:"x1", y1:"y1", x2:"x2", y2:"y2",
            stroke: "#c0392b", strokeWidth: 2, headLength: 14, headAngle: 40 }),
        isCounter
          ? Plot.arrow([{ x1: 0.52, y1: Tc_mid - arrowOff, x2: 0.38, y2: Tc_mid - arrowOff }],
              { x1:"x1", y1:"y1", x2:"x2", y2:"y2",
                stroke: "#2471a3", strokeWidth: 2, headLength: 14, headAngle: 40 })
          : Plot.arrow([{ x1: 0.38, y1: Tc_mid - arrowOff, x2: 0.52, y2: Tc_mid - arrowOff }],
              { x1:"x1", y1:"y1", x2:"x2", y2:"y2",
                stroke: "#2471a3", strokeWidth: 2, headLength: 14, headAngle: 40 }),

        // ΔT vertical brackets at each end
        Plot.link(
          [{ x1:0, y1:Thin,  x2:0, y2:Tc0  },
           { x1:1, y1:Thout, x2:1, y2:Tc1  }],
          { x1:"x1", y1:"y1", x2:"x2", y2:"y2",
            stroke: "#27ae60", strokeWidth: 2, strokeDasharray: "4,2" }
        ),

        // ΔT labels between the lines at each end
        Plot.text([
          { xi: 0.04, T: (Thin  + Tc0)/2, lbl: `ΔT₁ = ${DT1.toFixed(1)} °C` },
          { xi: 0.96, T: (Thout + Tc1)/2, lbl: `ΔT₂ = ${DT2.toFixed(1)} °C` },
        ], {
          x: "xi", y: "T", text: "lbl",
          textAnchor: d => d.xi < 0.5 ? "start" : "end",
          fill: "#27ae60", fontSize: 10, fontFamily: "Georgia, serif",
        }),

        // Stream endpoint labels (small, near line ends — pushed inward to clear the ΔT brackets)
        Plot.text([
          { xi: 0.06, T: Thin,  lbl: isConstantTs ? "Tₛ"    : "Hot in",   col: "#c0392b", anc: "start" },
          { xi: 0.94, T: Thout, lbl: isConstantTs ? "Tₛ"    : "Hot out",  col: "#c0392b", anc: "end"   },
          { xi: isCounter ? 0.94 : 0.06,
            T:  isCounter ? Tc1  : Tc0,
            lbl: isConstantTs ? "T_in"  : "Cold in",  col: "#2471a3", anc: isCounter ? "end" : "start" },
          { xi: isCounter ? 0.06 : 0.94,
            T:  isCounter ? Tc0  : Tc1,
            lbl: isConstantTs ? "T_out" : "Cold out", col: "#2471a3", anc: isCounter ? "start" : "end" },
        ], {
          x: "xi", y: "T", text: "lbl",
          textAnchor: d => d.anc,
          fill: d => d.col, dy: -11,
          fontSize: 10, fontFamily: "Georgia, serif",
        }),

      ] : [
        Plot.text([{ xi: 0.5, T: 50 }], {
          x: "xi", y: "T",
          text: () => "⚠  Invalid — hot must exceed cold at both ends",
          fill: "#e15759", fontSize: 12,
        }),
      ]
    });

    // Summary panel
    const fmt1 = v => v.toFixed(1);
    const pct  = valid
      ? ((DT_arith - DT_lm) / DT_lm * 100).toFixed(1)
      : "—";
    pInfo.innerHTML = valid ? `
      <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr);gap:8px;
                  margin-top:10px;font-family:Georgia,serif;font-size:12px">
        <div style="text-align:center;padding:8px 4px;background:#f0f9f0;
                    border:1.5px solid #27ae60;border-radius:5px">
          <div style="color:#27ae60;font-weight:bold;margin-bottom:3px">ΔT₁</div>
          <div style="font-size:15px">${fmt1(DT1)} °C</div>
        </div>
        <div style="text-align:center;padding:8px 4px;background:#f0f9f0;
                    border:1.5px solid #27ae60;border-radius:5px">
          <div style="color:#27ae60;font-weight:bold;margin-bottom:3px">ΔT₂</div>
          <div style="font-size:15px">${fmt1(DT2)} °C</div>
        </div>
        <div style="text-align:center;padding:8px 4px;background:#e8f5e8;
                    border:2px solid #1e8449;border-radius:5px">
          <div style="color:#1e8449;font-weight:bold;margin-bottom:3px">ΔT<sub>lm</sub></div>
          <div style="font-size:18px;font-weight:bold">${fmt1(DT_lm)} °C</div>
        </div>
        <div style="text-align:center;padding:8px 4px;background:#f5f5f5;
                    border:1px solid #ccc;border-radius:5px">
          <div style="color:#999;font-weight:bold;margin-bottom:3px">Arith. mean</div>
          <div style="font-size:15px;color:#999">${fmt1(DT_arith)} °C</div>
          <div style="font-size:9px;color:#bbb;margin-top:2px">
            +${pct}% vs ΔT<sub>lm</sub>
          </div>
        </div>
      </div>` : "";

    pPlot.replaceChildren(plot);
  }

  for (const inp of [flow_in, Thin_in, Thout_in, Tcin_in, Tcout_in])
    inp.addEventListener("input", render);
  hIsoCb.addEventListener("change", render);
  cIsoCb.addEventListener("change", render);
  render();

  // ── Legend ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  const sw = (c) => {
    const s = document.createElement("span");
    s.style.cssText = `display:inline-block;width:22px;height:0;border-top:3px solid ${c};` +
                      `vertical-align:middle;margin:0 5px 0 12px`;
    return s;
  };
  const t = txt => { const s = document.createElement("span"); s.textContent = txt; return s; };
  const leg = document.createElement("div");
  leg.style.cssText = "font-size:11px;font-family:Georgia,serif;margin:8px 0 4px;" +
                      "display:flex;align-items:center;flex-wrap:wrap;gap:3px";
  leg.append(sw("#c0392b"), t("Hot stream (→)"),
             sw("#2471a3"), t("Cold stream (→ parallel / ← counter)"),
             sw("#27ae60"), t("ΔT₁, ΔT₂"));

  return html`<div style="font-family:Georgia,serif">

    <div style="margin-bottom:14px">${flow_in}</div>

    <div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;gap:14px;margin-bottom:16px">
      <div style="padding:10px 12px;border:1px solid #f0c0b0;border-radius:6px;
                  background:#fdf8f7">
        ${hotPanelLabel}
        ${Thin_in} ${Thout_in} ${hIsoCb}
      </div>
      <div style="padding:10px 12px;border:1px solid #a0c0e0;border-radius:6px;
                  background:#f7fafd">
        <div style="font-size:0.72em;font-weight:bold;color:#2471a3;
                    text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;margin-bottom:7px">
          Cold stream
        </div>
        ${Tcin_in} ${Tcout_in} ${cIsoCb}
      </div>
    </div>

    ${pPlot}
    ${leg}
    ${pInfo}
  </div>`;
}
```

---

## What to look for {#sec-observations}

**The tube bank: constant $T_s$.** The default view shows exactly the setup from the chapter — the tube surface is at constant $T_s$ (flat red line), and the external flow heats up exponentially from $T_\text{in}$ to $T_\text{out}$. Try setting $T_s = 70\,^\circ$C, $T_\text{in} = 15\,^\circ$C, $T_\text{out} = 41\,^\circ$C to match the worked example below and confirm $\Delta T_{lm} \approx 40.6\,^\circ$C.

::: {.callout-note}
A constant-temperature surface can occur during a phase change, and it also approximates any stream with a very large heat capacity rate $C = \dot{m}c_p$: if enough fluid is flowing, its temperature barely shifts and it looks flat on the plot.
:::

**Arithmetic mean always overestimates.** The summary panel shows the arithmetic mean alongside $\Delta T_{lm}$. The arithmetic mean is always greater than or equal to $\Delta T_{lm}$. Using it in $\dot{Q} = \overline{h}A\,\Delta T$ would overestimate the heat transfer rate. The correction matters most when $\Delta T_1 / \Delta T_2$ is far from 1.

**Counter flow outperforms parallel flow — with the same terminal temperatures.** *(Coming in a few weeks, but worth a peek now.)* Switch to "Counter flow" or "Parallel flow" with $T_h$: 90 → 60 °C, $T_c$: 20 → 55 °C. The arithmetic mean $\Delta T$ is the same for both — it only depends on the four terminal values. But $\Delta T_{lm}$ is notably higher for counter flow, because counter flow keeps $\Delta T_1$ and $\Delta T_2$ more balanced; the log-mean penalizes large imbalance. That gap is the engineering motivation for counter-flow designs.

**When $\Delta T_1 = \Delta T_2$**, $\Delta T_{lm} = \Delta T_1$ exactly. (To show this, remember L'Hôpital's Rule.) The profiles become straight lines and the arithmetic mean is correct. This happens in counter flow when the two streams have equal capacity rates — a balanced, perfectly efficient exchanger.

---

## Worked example — tube bank as isothermal hot surface {#sec-worked}

A bank of $N_T = 4$ tubes wide and $N_L = 20$ rows deep ($N = 80$ total) is arranged in an aligned square pitch: $D = 0.025$ m, $S_T = S_L = 0.050$ m, tube length $L = 1$ m. Air approaches at $V_\infty = 4$ m/s and $T_\text{in} = 15\,^\circ$C; the tubes are held at $T_s = 70\,^\circ$C. Find $\overline{h}$, the outlet temperature, $\Delta T_{lm}$, and the total heat transfer.

**Step 1 — Find $\overline{h}$ from the tube bank correlation.**

We need properties at $T_\text{avg} = (T_\text{in} + T_\text{out})/2$, but we don't know $T_\text{out}$ yet. We'll guess $T_\text{out} \approx 40\,^\circ$C, putting $T_\text{avg} \approx 27\,^\circ$C $\approx 300$ K, and check afterward. Air properties at 300 K (from Wolfram Alpha): $k = 0.0264\;\text{W/m·K}$, $\nu = 15.7\times 10^{-6}\;\text{m}^2\text{/s}$, $\rho = 1.18\;\text{kg/m}^3$, $c_p = 1005\;\text{J/kg·K}$, $\text{Pr} = 0.707$. At $T_s = 70\,^\circ$C: $\text{Pr}_s = 0.702$.

Maximum velocity through the narrowest cross-section between tubes:

$$V_\text{max} = \frac{S_T}{S_T - D}\,V_\infty = \frac{0.050}{0.050 - 0.025}\times 4 = 8\;\text{m/s}$$

$$\text{Re}_{D,\max} = \frac{V_\text{max}\,D}{\nu} = \frac{8\times 0.025}{15.7\times10^{-6}} = 12{,}700$$

This falls in the range $10^3$–$2\times10^5$ for an aligned bank with $S_T/S_L = 1.0 > 0.7$, so from the table: $C_1 = 0.27$, $m = 0.63$. Since $N_L = 20$, no correction factor is needed ($C_2 = 1$). The Prandtl ratio $(0.707/0.702)^{1/4} \approx 1.00$ — essentially no effect for air — so:

$$\overline{\text{Nu}}_D = 0.27\times 12700^{0.63}\times 0.707^{0.36} = 0.27\times 385\times 0.883 \approx 92.0$$

$$\overline{h} = \frac{k}{D}\,\overline{\text{Nu}}_D = \frac{0.0264}{0.025}\times 92.0 \approx 97\;\text{W/m}^2\text{K}$$

**Step 2 — Estimate the outlet temperature.**

$$\frac{T_s - T_\text{out}}{T_s - T_\text{in}} = \exp\!\left(\frac{-\pi D N\,\overline{h}}{\rho\,V_\infty\,N_T\,S_T\,c_p}\right) = \exp\!\left(\frac{-\pi\times 0.025\times 80\times 97}{1.18\times 4\times 4\times 0.050\times 1005}\right) = \exp(-0.642) = 0.526$$

$$T_\text{out} = 70 - 0.526\times(70-15) = 70 - 28.9 \approx 41\,^\circ\text{C}$$

$T_\text{avg} = (15 + 41)/2 = 28\,^\circ\text{C} \approx 300$ K — consistent with our property choice. ✓

**Step 3 — Apply the log-mean formula.**

$$\Delta T_1 = T_s - T_\text{in} = 70 - 15 = 55\,^\circ\text{C}, \qquad \Delta T_2 = T_s - T_\text{out} = 70 - 41 = 29\,^\circ\text{C}$$

$$\Delta T_{lm} = \frac{55 - 29}{\ln(55/29)} = \frac{26}{\ln 1.897} = \frac{26}{0.640} = 40.6\,^\circ\text{C}$$

Check against the demo: in the default "Constant $T_s$" mode, set $T_s = 70\,^\circ$C, $T_\text{in} = 15\,^\circ$C, $T_\text{out} = 41\,^\circ$C. The summary should read $\Delta T_{lm} \approx 40.6\,^\circ$C.

The arithmetic mean gives $(55 + 29)/2 = 42\,^\circ$C — about 3% above $\Delta T_{lm}$ here. Not catastrophic, but the error grows quickly as $\Delta T_1/\Delta T_2$ moves away from 1.

**Step 4 — Total heat transfer:**

$$\dot{Q} = N\,\overline{h}\,\pi D L\,\Delta T_{lm} = 80\times 97\times\pi\times 0.025\times 1\times 40.6 \approx 24.7\;\text{kW}$$
